entry.title: FioriElements - ToolTip for a FieldGroup - Table-Column
entry.content:
Hi FioriElements - experts,

I'm a Fiori Elements newbe and work on my first App. The App displays all kind of information from SAP BP, SAP-FSCD (insurance) and DWH.
Data are coming from CDS-Views. The annotations are done in both, CDS and XML, depending on where it was easier to achieve…

Most things are fairly easy/straight forward and work fine.

However, i'm struggeling with a couple of issue. I found information in the community, but they did not work… 😞  So i decided to bring them up here, hoping to find some advice.
Thanks in advance for any response!
Best regards from Germany
Matthias

Here is the list of all issues. So please check my other posts as well 🙂

ListReport - relatet:
1. Value-Help that does not load the data immediately
ObjectPage - relatet:
2. Dynamic SemanticObject (for SemanticNavigation)
3. Display Labels for a ColumnMicroChart
4. Grouping of table-entries
5. Two tables per section with CollectionFacet-annotation
6. ToolTip for a FieldGroup - Table-Column 

---------------------------------

6. ToolTip for a FieldGroup - Table-Column

I like to offer ToolTips for each TableColumn.

For a standard column, it works fine by adding the CDS-annotation: @EndUserText.quickInfo

However, I don't achieve ToolTips for a column, that consists of a FieldGroup.
1) in CDS, there is no such annotation.
2) XML-Annotation on Field-Group-Level has no effect: <Annotation Term="Common.QuickInfo" String="Vertragsnummern"/>
3) Common.QuickInfo for DataField does not exist

entry.title: Recap SAP Stammtisch - Zukunftstrends
entry.content:
Der letzte SAP Stammtisch Magdeburg stand ganz im Zeichen der Zukunftstrends! 🌟 Hierfür gab es drei spannende Impulsvorträge sowie angeregte Diskussionen:

Jörg Müller von BA Business Advice GmbH berichtete vom Zukunftstag Produktion im Fraunhofer IFF als Ersatzveranstaltung zum ursprünglich geplanten Barcamp. (Welches übrigens erneut für Q3 geplant ist, also bleiben Sie gespannt! 🔄) Neben Vorträgen zu den Themen Connected Factory, Machine Learning und SAP Apps in Produktion und Logistik war ein Rundgang durch die Elbfabrik ein echtes Highlight. 🏭

@dr. Robert Neumann von Ultra Tendency stellte zunächst die Firma und Databricks vor. Diese Plattform bietet leistungsstarke Werkzeuge für BigData und KI-gestützte Analysen. Besonders spannend wurde es, als der Launch von SAP Databricks angekündigt wurde, gefolgt von einer beeindruckenden Live-Demo. 🚀🔍

Stefan Weidner vom UCC Magdeburg präsentierte das neue EHS Modul in SAP S/4HANA sowie das neue Sustainability Curriculum des UCC. Damit ist es möglich, den Carbon Footprint eines (fiktiven) Events im SAP S/4HANA-System abzubilden und diesen in der SAP Analytics Cloud grafisch darzustellen. 🌍📊

Vielen Dank an alle Vortragenden für die spannenden Inputs und an alle Gäste für die interessanten Diskussionen! Wir freuen uns auf das nächste Mal, Infos folgen. 📅

________

The last SAP Stammtisch Magdeburg was all about future trends! 🌟 There were three exciting keynote speeches and lively discussions:

Jörg Müller from BA Business Advice GmbH reported on the Future Production Day at the Fraunhofer IFF as a replacement event for the originally planned Barcamp. (Which, by the way, is planned again for Q3, so stay tuned! 🔄) In addition to presentations on the topics of connected factory, machine learning and SAP apps in production and logistics, a tour of the Elbfabrik was a real highlight. 🏭

Dr. Robert Neumann from Ultra Tendency first introduced the company and Databricks. This platform offers powerful tools for big data and AI-supported analyses. Things got particularly exciting when the launch of SAP Databricks was announced, followed by an impressive live demo. 🚀🔍

Stefan Weidner from UCC Magdeburg presented the new EHS module in SAP S/4HANA and UCC's new Sustainability Curriculum. This makes it possible to map the carbon footprint of a (fictitious) event in the SAP S/4HANA system and display it graphically in the SAP Analytics Cloud. 🌍📊

Many thanks to all speakers for the exciting inputs and to all guests for the interesting discussions! We look forward to the next event, information to follow. 📅

entry.title: Re: Delta processing inside SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@Wanda_Soetomo first of all thanks for this blog. 

Correct me if i understood it wrong, but you still have to do a full load in your staging table. Have you maybe tried to use this logic on the remote table directly? And if yes are the filters pushed down to the source? Because nevertheless the first step will be a full load and if there are many records you still can have performance issues.

entry.title: Re: SBOP BI Platform 4.3 SP04 Patch 10: Logon failed for...
entry.content:
We got help from SAP Support:

Copy the Tomcat (or load balancer) https URL into the browser address bar:

Note: it must be the same URL the users use to access the system

Press enter to navigate to this URL

Click , next to the URL

A window should open with the certificate details (Certificate Viewer)

Select the 'Details' tab

Click 'Export'

Save the certificate, but change the format to DER Encoded Binary (.der)

Click Save

Copy the exported certificate to the machine(s) where Tomcat is located

 

Then follow the steps below 'Installing the Certificate

 

Open a command prompt

Navigate to the following location: cd C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64\sapjvm\bin

Run

keytool -import -noprompt -trustcacerts -alias <uniquealias> -file <path to .der file> -keystore ../jre/lib/security/cacerts -storepass changeit

e.g. keytool -import -noprompt -trustcacerts -alias CTSPM -file c:/ssl/tomcatcert.der -keystore ../jre/lib/security/cacerts -storepass changeit

Also Add the below parameter in tomcat configuration.

 -Djdk.tls.client.protocols=TLSv1.2

Restart Tomcat

entry.title: SAC Selection Type in SortByValue
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

Currently i am trying to have a switch sort my table desecending on two columns(using Meassures) depending on wether the switch in on or off, now i am using the sortByValue from SAC in which you have to give relatedDimensions of type Selection, SortOrder and Direction i have figured out what i need for the latter two but i dont know what or how i can fill the relatedDimensions.

sortByValue function

Also, is this the correct function or am I on the wrong path, if so, please provide the correct path.

Thank you all and Best Regards
Paul

entry.title: Re: Edocument framework installiation 3005076 note delete...
entry.content: Hi Mate, Please Retrieve / Copy the CL_EDOCUMENT CLASS from the QA system to Dev System then de-implement the NOTE 3005076. it will work.

entry.title: Best Practices for SAP HANA DB Installation on SUSE Linux
entry.content:
I am planning to install SAP HANA DB on SUSE Linux and would appreciate your insights on the best practices for this process. Specifically, I have the following questions:

  1. Installation Sequence: Should I install the Linux operating system first and then install SAP HANA, or is it better to install SUSE SLE 15 and SAP components simultaneously during the OS installation?

  2. Important Components: Which SAP components are crucial to install during the initial setup? If any components are missed during the initial installation, can they be added later? If so, how can I add these missed components or add-ons?

entry.content: Basically, you want to create a wizard for Manageing Database Users ?

I Developed on sticker printing ui5 application which print the smartform. but in this I can show the pdf preview through Get~stream DPC_EXT method, but when I want to print on output device its not sending any print on output device (printer). I used post method for smartform print. experts, I want your valuable help on this issue.

entry.title: Re: Create ABAP program to send SQL command to another HA...
entry.content:
Basically, you want to create a cockpit/wizard for Managing Database Users ?

  • Look at attachment of notes such as 1986645 - Allow only administration users to work on HANA database to get an idea of the SQL statements to be executed.
  • Insure that SAP HANA Database Administration with SAP HANA Cockpit isn't the solution

entry.title: Re: BADI not found in Delivery note item level
entry.content:
Hi jpriya,

I am not understanding your comment.

You indicated you implemented the Header level BAdI and asked where to find the Item level one.

I provided the screenshot showing the item level one and also provided the help link to the list of the all the available BAdIs, which includes the item level one.

I also indicated that the BAdI would not likely be triggered in the Display Outbound Delivery app as there are no changes done with that app.

If you could clarify which BAdI you implemented, and how you are trying to trigger it, we could possibly provide more help.

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: BTP ABAP Environment Fiori Launchpad Missing Administrator Space
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We have created our first application on the BTP ABAP Environment Trial Account and successfully deployed it. However, when we try to add this application to the launchpad, we are unable to find the administrator space and related tools.

We have checked all user settings on the BTP Trial account, but nothing has changed.

Could you please help us resolve this issue?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Deployment Scheduling in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi @anton_lundovskiy1,

Thanks for the question and your interest in the Deployment Scheduler.

To be precise this is about project access restrictions and for private projects you need to be part of the project team to access it. The mentioned note is referring to following case:

User #1 with Deployment Manager role collection assigned creates a deployment schedule for a deployment plan assigned to a private project. To create the schedule User #1 must be assigned to the private project otherwise an error message will block the creation. But if User #1 is assigned to the private project the schedule can be created.

In case the project lead of the private project is removing User #1 from the team member list at a later point in time this will not affect the existing deployment schedule. Access to the private project is only necessary when creating or editing a deployment schedule. As an alternative a Project Admin can access all projects and would not encounter such an issue.

I have adapted the note in the blog post. 

Does this answer your question?

Best regards,

Moritz

entry.title: Re: The data sometimes comes out weird in the Story Report.
entry.content: Hi Gloria, I am reaching out for guidance on the LMS Standard Item Status Story iReport, which I have been working on recently. I imported the SFLMS Item Status version 1.0 from the Content Store, but the data appears to be inconsistent. As shown in the attached image, the item report has five pages, with the first page displaying "Due Soon" data. However, the calculation for "Items Assigned to Learners" shows 1092, which is derived from the major aggregate count of the dimension using different columns. I am struggling to understand the logic behind this calculation and would greatly appreciate any guidance or clarification on this matter. Unfortunately, there are no other reports available in the LMS system to cross-verify or validate the data. Please find the attached image for reference. I look forward to hearing from you and resolving this issue.

entry.title: Re: Purchase order creation failed for freight settlement document 9xxxxx
entry.content: Dear good day, I have the same error, what maintenance did you do to the PO document?

entry.title: Re: Restrictions in app Profit Centers - Actuals
entry.content:
Thank you for the insights. After further investigation, we encountered a limitation when trying to restrict the Profit Centers - Actuals app by company code. Specifically: If we set the restriction to Restricted for a company code, the system cannot access the ledgers, resulting in an empty report.

If we allow access to the ledgers, all company codes become visible, defeating the purpose of the restriction. This behavior is different from how company code restrictions work for other objects, such as Plants, where limitations function correctly. Given this limitation, is there any alternative approach to achieving company code-specific restrictions in Profit Centers - Actuals?

Alternatively, is there another reporting app that provides profit center actuals while allowing company code-based access control? Any insights or workarounds would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: SAP Screen Personas 3.0 SP18 Flavor freezes
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a problem with SAP Screen Personas , and I don't know what to do. I have created a flavor for the transaction BP with the Personas setting  ENABLE_BDT_MODE is set to X like mentioned in Solved: Screen Personas SP18 transport from one system to ... - SAP Community.

This time, however, I have the problem that the flavor freezes every time I want to set an entry for the tax numbers with the input help ( F4 help ).

 

At this point, the flavor freezes and I can no longer do anything. I have to reload the whole page and all the data I entered before is no longer available.
I work with scripts that are located at onAfterRefresh

If I remove this script there, the functionality is there, but the problem is that this script is mandatory with this onAfterRefresh.
I also used 

session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey(0); to maybe unfreeze the Flavor, but that didn't work.

Do you have any Idea how to fix this ?

Best regards Dominik

entry.title: Re: SUP AATP
entry.content:
Did ou already check

  1. in online help - CA-ATP-SUP vertical horizontal 
  2. in OSS notes - 2601288 - FMS EHP8 SP10: Stock Protection standardization in ATP

entry.title: 在启用高级评估之后，计提坏账准备时，需要勾选客户主数据中的信用参数文件的风险类，导致必须启用信用额度；不勾选风险类，进行基于信用风险的减值计提时，不能计提；
entry.content: #SAP S4HANA Cloud #SAP S4HANA Cloud Public#SAP S4HANA Cloud Public Edition#SAP S4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance#SAP S4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance#在启用高级评估之后，计提坏账准备时（即基于信用风险的减值），就必须需要勾选客户主数据中的信用参数文件的风险类，导致必须启用信用额度，否则销售订单时，显示信用额度关闭；不勾选风险类，进行基于信用风险的减值计提时，不能计提；实际业务中，未启用信用额度，但是要进行坏账计提，如何处理这种冲突业务？

entry.title: I am reaching out for guidance on the LMS Standard Item Status Story iReport, which I have been wor
entry.content: @gloriakurz  and whole team, I am reaching out for guidance on the LMS Standard Item Status Story iReport, which I have been working on recently. I imported the SFLMS Item Status version 1.0 from the Content Store, but the data appears to be inconsistent. As shown in the attached image, the item report has five pages, with the first page displaying "Due Soon" data. However, the calculation for "Items Assigned to Learners" shows 1092, which is derived from the major aggregate count of the dimension using different columns. I am struggling to understand the logic behind this calculation and would greatly appreciate any guidance or clarification on this matter. Unfortunately, there are no other reports available in the LMS system to cross-verify or validate the data. Please find the attached image for reference. I look forward to hearing from you and resolving this issue.

entry.title: Re: Transaction Type OF OINM TABLE
entry.content: THANK YOU SIR

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price changing after procurement of fr...
entry.content:
Hello @Aicha_000 

As per SAP Glossary moving average price

A price that changes in consequence of goods movements and the entry of invoices and is used to valuate a material. The moving average price is calculated by dividing the value of the material by the quantity of material in stock. It is automatically recalculated by the system after each goods movement or invoice entry.

Therefore, when a free goods are received the total value of the stock goes down and thus the moving average goes down. That is not just an SAP best practice, that is common sense.

You could use split valuation to separate free goods separately, however the total moving average price of the material will still be reduced.

What is a rationale for the requirement?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How to Add attachments from GOS to class based workfl...
entry.content: Look at this sample from support: Using SOFM Object for Adding GOS Attachments to Work item

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to handle grandfathered cases in the a...
entry.content:
Thanks for the reply Xavier !!

Even if we create custom fields and business rules in EC, over a period of time these grandfathered cased will be normalised and later we will not need all these custom things and all employees will eventually fall under the standard rules.

entry.title: Visualizing Your SAP Bill of Materials Using CAP, Fiori elements, and the UI5 Network Graph
entry.content:
In this blog post, I want to demonstrate how to create a simple Bill of Materials (BOM) relationship visualization using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) and consume it in a SAP Fiori Elements application. We will display the BOM structure as a Network Graph, making it easy for end users to navigate parent/child relationships among materials.

1. Reason and Basic Setup

When handling production or manufacturing processes, a Bill of Materials (BOM) is one of the fundamental structures that define how materials are composed or assembled. By visualizing the BOM in a graph, it is much easier to understand complex hierarchies.

For this example, I am using an SAP CAP project that manages Materials and their BOM structures, along with a pseudo-entity GraphNetwork that dynamically assembles the data for a SAP sap.suite.ui.commons.networkgraph control to consume.
You can see more examples of the NetworkGraph in the UI5 Documentation.

The example is a very simplified example with a self-created schema in CAP.
Theoretically, this should of course also be possible in ABAP using the SAP tables as a basis.
In CAP, this is of course also possible if CAP has access to the standard SAP tables.
The aim here is not to show a productive application but only an MVP to show that this is possible.

Below is our simplified “Material BOM” setup. Each Material can have multiple child Materials (via MaterialBOM entries), and each relationship can have additional attributes like quantity, unit of measure, or relationship type (e.g., “Component,” “Raw,” or “Packaging”).

We will walk through the overall CAP schema, a custom read API to generate the network data, and finally a Fiori Elements application that displays the BOM as a network graph.

You find the complete project in my GitHub Repository: https://github.com/marianfoo/cap-materialbom-graph 

2. Create CAP Schema

Below is the full schema definition in schema.cds. It includes the entities: Material, MaterialBOM, RelationshipType, and the pseudo-entity GraphNetwork used to deliver JSON node/line structures. I've added additional comments to help clarify each part:

 

namespace de.marianzeis.materialbomgraph;
using { cuid, managed } from '@sap/cds/common';

/**
 * The 'Material' entity represents a product or raw material that can be part of a BOM.
 * It includes references to child and parent BOM compositions, as well as an embedded
 * GraphNetwork composition for the visualization.
 */
@odata.draft.enabled
@assert.unique: { materialId: [materialId] }
entity Material : cuid, managed {
  materialId: String(20) @mandatory;         // An internal ID (e.g., "KIT-001")
  materialDescription: String(100);          // A text field describing the material
  materialType: String(15);                  // Defines if it's "KIT", "COMPONENT", etc.
  
  // Composition to build or retrieve the GraphNetwork for this Material
  graph : Composition of one GraphNetwork on graph.root = $self;

  // BOM references: 
  // The parentMaterial or childMaterial in 'MaterialBOM' associations 
  // form the link in the hierarchy.
  bomParent: Composition of many MaterialBOM on bomParent.parentMaterial = $self;
  bomChild: Composition of many MaterialBOM on bomChild.childMaterial = $self;
}

/**
 * 'MaterialBOM' is a bridging entity to define parent-child relationships. 
 * It stores the quantity, unit of measure, and an associated relationship type.
 */
entity MaterialBOM : cuid, managed {
  parentMaterial: Association to Material;   // e.g., higher-level component
  childMaterial: Association to Material;    // e.g., sub-component, raw, or packaging
  quantity: Decimal(13,3);                  // e.g., "2.5 childMaterial units"
  uom: String(10);                          // e.g., "KG", "L", "PC"
  relationshipType: Association to one RelationshipType;
}

/**
 * 'RelationshipType' might define how the child is used: 'Component', 'Packaging', 'Raw', etc.
 */
@assert.unique: { code: [code] }
@odata.draft.enabled
entity RelationshipType : cuid, managed {
  code: String(10) @mandatory;
  name: String(50);
}

/**
 * The 'GraphNetwork' entity returns "nodes" and "lines" as JSON strings
 * so we can display a network diagram in the front-end (sap.suite.ui.commons.networkgraph).
 * 'root' links back to the parent Material for clarity. 'groups' is optional
 * for advanced grouping or clustering of nodes.
 */
entity GraphNetwork {
  key ID: UUID;
  root: Association to Material;
  nodes: LargeString; 
  lines: LargeString; 
  groups: LargeString; 
}

 

3. Create CAP GraphNetwork READ API

Next, we need to define a service that exposes our entities (Material, MaterialBOM, RelationshipType, and GraphNetwork). In our service.cds, we create a service named MaterialGraph:

 

using {de.marianzeis.materialbomgraph as MaterialSchema} from '../db/schema.cds';

service MaterialGraph {
    entity Material         as projection on MaterialSchema.Material;
    entity MaterialBOM      as projection on MaterialSchema.MaterialBOM;
    entity RelationshipType as projection on MaterialSchema.RelationshipType;
    entity GraphNetwork     as
        projection on MaterialSchema.GraphNetwork {
            *,
            root : redirected to Material
        };
}

 

This makes all four entities available as OData V4 endpoints. The GraphNetwork entity is “pseudo” in that it doesn’t hold data in the database itself; it is populated at runtime in the service implementation.
We don´t use "@CDS.persistence.skip", as this would lead to errors on the Fiori elements object page, when going into edit mode.

The main logic for generating the network data (JSON nodes and lines) is found in service.js.
I return JSON because the structure here is more flexible for the graph in the frontend. This then requires a little extra code in the frontend with a little more flexibility.
It performs a recursive traversal of BOM structures, starting from a given “root” material. We use sets and maps to avoid loops and to accumulate node/edge data. Below is the expanded implementation with extra comments:

 

const cds = require('@sap/cds');

module.exports = cds.service.impl(function () {
  const { Material, MaterialBOM, RelationshipType } = this.entities;

  /**
   * Overwrite READ for the pseudo-entity "GraphNetwork".
   * We'll accept a Material's ID as the "root" in the request path, for example:
   * GET /GraphNetwork(ID=<material-uuid>, IsActiveEntity=true)
   */
  this.on('READ', 'GraphNetwork', async (req) => {
    // 1) Extract the root Material's ID from the request parameters.
    const rootMaterialID = req.params[0]?.ID;
    if (!rootMaterialID) {
      // If no root ID is found, return an empty array,
      // meaning we have no graph to build.
      return [];
    }

    // 2) We'll perform a Depth-First Search (DFS) or Breadth-First Search (BFS)
    // to gather child materials and build a graph of nodes and edges.
    // visited: keeps track of visited nodes to prevent infinite loops.
    // nodesMap: a Map storing node definitions for each material.
    // linesArr: an array of edges connecting parent -> child materials.
    const visited = new Set();
    const nodesMap = new Map();
    const linesArr = [];

    // 'traverseBOM' recursively explores child materials.
    // 'path' helps ensure unique node keys when the same child is reached via different paths.
    async function traverseBOM(parentID, path = '') {
      // Each visitedKey is a combination of the current parentID + path.
      const visitedKey = \`\${parentID}_\${path}\`;
      if (visited.has(visitedKey)) return;
      visited.add(visitedKey);

      // Fetch the parent material record, containing IDs, descriptions, etc.
      const parentMat = await SELECT.one.from(Material).where({ ID: parentID });
      if (!parentMat) return;

      // Create a unique node key. For example, "KIT-001_" or "RAW-001_KIT-001_"...
      const nodeKey = \`\${parentMat.materialId}_\${path}\`;

      // Insert/update node details in the nodesMap.
      // This node object is read by the sap.suite.ui.commons.networkgraph component.
      nodesMap.set(nodeKey, {
        key: nodeKey,
        title: parentMat.materialDescription,
        icon: parentMat.materialType,         // e.g., "KIT", "COMPONENT", "RAW_MATERIAL"
        status: parentMat.materialType,       // used for color-coding
        attributes: [
          { label: 'Material ID', value: parentMat.materialId },
          { label: 'Material Type', value: parentMat.materialType }
        ],
        customData: {
          materialId: parentMat.materialId,
          materialType: parentMat.materialType,
          material_ID: parentMat.ID
        }
      });

      // Look up child BOMs for this parent (meaning: which materials does the parent reference?)
      const childBOMs = await SELECT.from(MaterialBOM).where({ parentMaterial_ID: parentID });
      for (const row of childBOMs) {
        // For each child entry, fetch the child's record from 'Material'
        const childMat = await SELECT.one.from(Material).where({ ID: row.childMaterial_ID });
        if (childMat) {
          // Build the node key for the child. We append the parent's materialId to 'path'
          // to ensure uniqueness if a child is reached multiple times from different branches.
          const childNodeKey = \`\${childMat.materialId}_\${path}\${parentMat.materialId}_\`;

          // Retrieve the relationship type, e.g., "Component", "Filling Material", etc.
          const relationshipType = await SELECT.one.from(RelationshipType).where({ ID: row.relationshipType_ID });

          // Create a "line" (edge) from parent -> child in the graph.
          // 'title' is displayed near the line, and 'description' can be additional info (like quantity).
          linesArr.push({
            from: nodeKey,
            to: childNodeKey,
            description: \`Quantity: \${row.quantity} \${row.uom}\`,
            title: relationshipType.name
          });

          // Recursively traverse the child's BOM.
          await traverseBOM(childMat.ID, \`\${path}\${parentMat.materialId}_\`);
        }
      }
    }

    // Start traversal from the root material the user requested.
    await traverseBOM(rootMaterialID);

    // 3) Return exactly one record for GraphNetwork with the computed nodes and lines.
    // 'groups' can be used if we need to group or cluster certain materials.
    return [
      {
        ID: '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000', // a fixed placeholder ID
        root_ID: rootMaterialID,                    // which Material is "root" for this graph
        nodes: JSON.stringify(Array.from(nodesMap.values())),  // stringified array of node objects
        lines: JSON.stringify(linesArr)                         // stringified array of edges
      }
    ];
  });
});

 

With this, whenever we do a GET request such as:

 

GET /GraphNetwork(ID=<UUID>,IsActiveEntity=true)

 

... the CAP service will generate a list of “nodes” and “lines” in JSON for our network graph, enabling the front-end to easily bind it to the sap.suite.ui.commons.networkgraph control.

4. Consume in Fiori Elements App

To visualize the data, I have created an SAP Fiori Elements application using the Application Generator (“Fiori: Open Application Generator” in VS Code or SAP Business Application Studio). My app is TypeScript-based and includes a controller extension with a custom section to embed the Network Graph.

The key point is that from the Object Page, I fetch the GraphNetwork by calling materialID + '/graph' via OData. For example:

 

http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/material-graph/Material(
  ID=88888888-8888-8888-8888-888888888888, 
  IsActiveEntity=true
)/graph

 

This is a sample response of a a Material with two child materials:

 

{
  "@odata.context": "$metadata#GraphNetwork/$entity",
  "ID": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
  "root_ID": "88888888-8888-8888-8888-888888888888",
  "nodes": "[{\"key\":\"BLK-001_\",\"title\":\"Pre-Mixed Filter Solution\",\"icon\":\"BULK\",\"status\":\"BULK\",\"attributes\":[{\"label\":\"Material ID\",\"value\":\"BLK-001\"},{\"label\":\"Material Type\",\"value\":\"BULK\"}],\"customData\":{\"materialId\":\"BLK-001\",\"materialType\":\"BULK\",\"material_ID\":\"88888888-8888-8888-8888-888888888888\"}},{\"key\":\"ESS-001_BLK-001_\",\"title\":\"Mint Fresh Essence\",\"icon\":\"ESSENCE\",\"status\":\"ESSENCE\",\"attributes\":[{\"label\":\"Material ID\",\"value\":\"ESS-001\"},{\"label\":\"Material Type\",\"value\":\"ESSENCE\"}],\"customData\":{\"materialId\":\"ESS-001\",\"materialType\":\"ESSENCE\",\"material_ID\":\"55555555-5555-5555-5555-555555555555\"}},{\"key\":\"WAT-001_BLK-001_\",\"title\":\"Purified Water Base\",\"icon\":\"WATER\",\"status\":\"WATER\",\"attributes\":[{\"label\":\"Material ID\",\"value\":\"WAT-001\"},{\"label\":\"Material Type\",\"value\":\"WATER\"}],\"customData\":{\"materialId\":\"WAT-001\",\"materialType\":\"WATER\",\"material_ID\":\"66666666-6666-6666-6666-666666666666\"}}]",
  "lines": "[{\"from\":\"BLK-001_\",\"to\":\"ESS-001_BLK-001_\",\"description\":\"Quantity: 0.05 L\",\"title\":\"Filling Material\"},{\"from\":\"BLK-001_\",\"to\":\"WAT-001_BLK-001_\",\"description\":\"Quantity: 0.95 L\",\"title\":\"Filling Material\"}]",
  "HasActiveEntity": false,
  "HasDraftEntity": false,
  "IsActiveEntity": true
}

 

Because the GraphNetwork is associated to the Material entity, this navigation property automatically invokes the on('READ','GraphNetwork') logic we defined, giving us back our “nodes” and “lines” in JSON.

Below is the relevant section of my manifest.json that sets up the controller extension and configures a custom section for displaying the graph. I have omitted other parts for brevity:

 

{
  "sap.ui5": {
    "extends": {
      "extensions": {
        "sap.ui.controllerExtensions": {
          "sap.fe.templates.ObjectPage.ObjectPageController": {
            "controllerNames": [
              "de.marianzeis.material.ext.controller.ObjectPage"
            ]
          }
        }
      }
    },
    "routing": {
      "targets": {
        "MaterialObjectPage": {
          "options": {
            "settings": {
              "content": {
                "body": {
                  "sections": {
                    "myCustomSection": {
                      "template": "de.marianzeis.material.custom.fragment.graph",
                      "title": "Graph",
                      "position": {
                        "placement": "After",
                        "anchor": "MaterialBOM"
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

 

This creates a new custom section called “Graph” right after the standard BOM section in the Object Page. Inside that custom section, we will load our Network Graph from the graph.fragment.xml file.

5. Explain the Fragment and Controller Extension

In graph.fragment.xml, we define the sap.suite.ui.commons.networkgraph.Graph control, binding its nodes, lines, and optional groups to a graphModel JSON model. We also set up statuses for different material types, which color-code the nodes in the diagram. Below is an extended example with inline comments:

 

<core:FragmentDefinition xmlns:m="sap.m"
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns="sap.suite.ui.commons.networkgraph"
    xmlns:layout="sap.suite.ui.commons.networkgraph.layout">

    <!-- 
      The <Graph> control is the container for our network visualization.
      It binds to "graphModel" (nodes, lines, groups) that we populate in the controller.
    -->
    <Graph
        id="graph"
        busy="{graphSettings>/busy}"
        nodes="{graphModel>/nodes}"
        lines="{graphModel>/lines}"
        groups="{graphModel>/groups}"
        orientation="LeftRight"
        height="800px"
        enableWheelZoom="true">

        <!-- 
          Layout algorithm influences how nodes are placed.
          'LayeredLayout' with "LinearSegments" tries to lay them out in hierarchical layers.
        -->
        <layoutAlgorithm>
            <layout:LayeredLayout 
                mergeEdges="true" 
                nodeSpacing="75" 
                nodePlacement="LinearSegments" />
        </layoutAlgorithm>

        <!-- 
          'statuses' define color-coding, keyed by the "status" property 
          each node will have (e.g., "KIT", "COMPONENT", "RAW_MATERIAL").
        -->
        <statuses>
            <Status key="PACKAGING" backgroundColor="#bac8d3"/>
            <Status key="KIT" backgroundColor="#d5e8d4"/>
            <Status key="COMPONENT" backgroundColor="#5c9cd2"/>
            <Status key="RAW_MATERIAL" backgroundColor="#f9dad5"/>
            <Status key="ESSENCE" backgroundColor="#b1ddf0"/>
            <Status key="WATER" backgroundColor="#00b7c7"/>
            <Status key="BULK" backgroundColor="#fad7ac"/>
            <Status key="SALT_FILL" backgroundColor="#e3c800"/>
            <Status key="SALT_ASSEMBLY" backgroundColor="#DAA520"/>
            <Status key="LIQUID_FILL" backgroundColor="#dae8fc"/>
        </statuses>

        <!-- 
          'nodes' define the actual boxes or shapes for each material in the graph.
          We read "key", "title", "icon", "status" from graphModel, along with "attributes".
        -->
        <nodes>
            <Node
                shape="Box"
                key="{graphModel>key}"
                title="{graphModel>title}"
                icon="{graphModel>icon}"
                status="{graphModel>status}"
                showActionLinksButton="true"
                attributes="{ path:'graphModel>attributes' }">

                <!-- 
                  Each node can have multiple attributes, displayed on the UI as label:value.
                -->
                <attributes>
                    <ElementAttribute
                        label="{graphModel>label}"
                        value="{graphModel>value}" />
                </attributes>

                <!-- 
                  customData allows us to store the actual Material ID 
                  so we can navigate to the correct ObjectPage if the user clicks "Go to Material".
                -->
                <customData>
                    <core:CustomData key="materialId" value="{graphModel>customData/materialId}" />
                    <core:CustomData key="materialType" value="{graphModel>customData/materialType}" />
                    <core:CustomData key="material_ID" value="{graphModel>customData/material_ID}" />
                </customData>

                <!-- 
                  actionButtons can be placed on each node for custom interactions. 
                  Here, we provide a button to jump to the Material's details.
                -->
                <actionButtons>
                    <ActionButton
                        icon="sap-icon://chain-link"
                        title="Go to Material"
                        press=".extension.de.marianzeis.material.controller.ObjectPage.onGoToMaterial"/>
                </actionButtons>

            </Node>
        </nodes>

        <!-- 
          'lines' define connections (edges) between the nodes. 
          'from' and 'to' indicate node keys, while 'title' and 'description' 
          can carry text like "Component", "Quantity: 0.5 KG", etc.
        -->
        <lines>
            <Line 
              from="{graphModel>from}" 
              to="{graphModel>to}"
              description="{graphModel>description}" 
              title="{graphModel>title}" />
        </lines>

        <!-- 
          Optionally group sets of nodes visually. Not used in this basic example, 
          but we can define group keys in the same JSON if needed.
        -->
        <groups>
            <Group 
              key="{graphModel>key}" 
              title="{graphModel>title}" />
        </groups>

    </Graph>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

 

In the controller extension ObjectPage.controller.ts, we handle loading of the graph data after the user navigates to the Object Page, and also implement onGoToMaterial so the user can click a node’s action button to navigate to that material’s object page.

 

import ControllerExtension from 'sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension';
import ExtensionAPI from 'sap/fe/templates/ObjectPage/ExtensionAPI';
import JSONModel from 'sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel';
import { ActionButton$PressEvent } from 'sap/suite/ui/commons/networkgraph/ActionButton';
// ...

export default class ObjectPage extends ControllerExtension<ExtensionAPI> {
  static overrides = {
    /**
     * Called when the user navigates to the ObjectPage. We then load the graph 
     * for the currently bound Material context.
     */
    routing: {
      onAfterBinding: async function (bindingContext) {
        await this.loadGraph(bindingContext);
      }
    },

    /**
     * If using draft mode, after saving the object we can reload the graph to 
     * reflect changes to BOM relationships or material type, etc.
     */
    editFlow: {
      onAfterSave: async function (mParameters) {
        await this.loadGraph(mParameters.context);
      }
    }
  };

  /**
   * Action button event from each node: "Go to Material". 
   * We retrieve the customData to find the correct Material ID, 
   * then navigate to that object's detail page.
   */
  onGoToMaterial(this: ObjectPage, event: ActionButton$PressEvent): void {
    // Identify the node that triggered the button press
    const graph = this.getView().byId(
      'de.marianzeis.material::MaterialObjectPage--fe::CustomSubSection::myCustomSection--graph'
    );
    const nodes = graph.getNodes();
    const buttonNodeId = event.getParameter('id');

    // Attempt to match node ID with the trailing numeric part
    const graphNumber = buttonNodeId.split('-').pop();
    const node = nodes.find((node) => node.getId().endsWith(\`-\${graphNumber}\`));

    if (node) {
      // Extract customData to find the underlying Material's technical ID
      const customData = node.getCustomData();
      const material_ID = customData
        .find((d) => d.getKey() === 'material_ID')
        .getValue();

      // Build the binding path to that Material
      const model = this.base.getView().getModel();
      const bindingPath = \`/Material(ID=\${material_ID},IsActiveEntity=true)\`;
      const context = model.bindContext(bindingPath).getBoundContext();

      // Now navigate to the Material object page
      this.base.routing.navigate(context, { preserveHistory: true });
    }
  }

  /**
   * Loads the network graph data for the Material in the current context. 
   * This requests 'sPath + "/graph"' from the OData model, parses the returned 
   * JSON strings for nodes & lines, and sets the 'graphModel'.
   */
  private async loadGraph(context) {
    const view = this.getView();

    // Initialize a small "graphSettings" model to handle busy indicators, etc.
    if (!view.getModel('graphSettings')) {
      const graphSettings = new JSONModel({ busy: false, busyIndicatorDelay: 0 });
      view.setModel(graphSettings, 'graphSettings');
    }
    view.getModel('graphSettings').setProperty('/busy', true);

    // Build path to the 'graph' navigation property
    let sPath = context.getPath();
    // always load the data from the active entity
    sPath = sPath.replace(/IsActiveEntity=false/, 'IsActiveEntity=true');
    const oModel = view.getModel();

    try {
      // We bind to sPath + "/graph". Because of our CAP service, 
      // this will return an entity with 'nodes' and 'lines' as JSON strings.
      const oData = await oModel.bindContext(sPath + '/graph');
      await oData.requestObject();  // triggers the read
      const data = oData.getBoundContext().getObject();

      // Parse out nodes, lines, groups from the returned pseudo-entity
      const nodes = data.nodes ? JSON.parse(data.nodes) : [];
      const lines = data.lines ? JSON.parse(data.lines) : [];
      const groups = data.groups ? JSON.parse(data.groups) : [];

      const oJSONModel = new JSONModel({ nodes, lines, groups });
      // Increase size limit if the BOM is large
      oJSONModel.setSizeLimit(1000000);
      view.setModel(oJSONModel, 'graphModel');
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error loading graph data:', error);
    } finally {
      // Turn off the busy indicator
      view.getModel('graphSettings').setProperty('/busy', false);
    }
  }
}

 

As you can see, we fetch the network data (nodes/lines) by calling the “/graph” navigation property, then set it as a graphModel on the view. The Graph control in graph.fragment.xml binds to that model and automatically draws the diagram.

6. Show Screenshots of the Graph

Below are some sample screenshots. For example, if we open KIT-001 (Premium Water Filter Kit), the BOM includes packaging, components, raw materials, etc. The different colors correspond to distinct materialType categories, and the lines show the quantity plus the relationshipType (e.g., “Component,” “Filling Material,” “Packaging”).

Furthermore, more information can be added to each connection. Here we have added the relationship type and the weight.

As you can see, each node is clickable with an “Action Button” to navigate to the specific Material’s object page. This makes BOM exploration more intuitive and user-friendly.

Conclusion

We have successfully built a custom BOM Network Graph with CAP, generating the relationships dynamically from the MaterialBOM data and delivering it to a Fiori Elements Object Page. The sap.suite.ui.commons.networkgraph control offers multiple layout algorithms, status colors, custom attributes, and navigation actions, resulting in an interactive hierarchy view for end users.

Feel free to adapt these examples for your own projects, add grouping logic, or expand the relationships into multi-level BOMs. The approach remains the same: store the BOM, recursively gather the structure, and map it into the Network Graph “nodes” and “lines” format.

Further References

  • SAP CAP Documentation
  • SAPUI5 / Fiori Elements Documentation
  • sap.suite.ui.commons.networkgraph API

Thank you for reading, and happy coding!

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Situation type PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR not found
entry.content:
Check avaibility and requirements for your version at Manage Situation Types - Extended 

(and/or correct your tags)

entry.title: Re: Configure File Path for Payment Medium | Public Cloud
entry.content: Please, add a tag such as "SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" or "SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition" relevant for your system.

entry.title: Re: 10 Steps to Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for Customers

entry.title: Re: Upload Prices in Sales from template-based spreadsheet
entry.content: Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence - Request created. Everyone who has fields missing for the template download can add it in the comments of the request, so the team know which fields are being used and needs GFNs. If the request gets more votes, it moves up the priority list.

entry.title: Re: How to remove the delete option from Personal Data Co...
entry.content:
Hi

We just went live and brought upp the same problem, unfortunately this is a system limitation currently...

We created a workaround with Business Rules, have put one example as an attachment

entry.title: Availability check on MO with open OBD
entry.content:
Available qty: 2 EA

MO requirement qty: 2 EA

When the MO is created, the ATP commits the MO line with the available qty.

Once Outbound delivery is created for the MO, the material is allocated to OBD. ATP availablity check decommits the MO line.

entry.title: Re: Deleting Logical system from BD54
entry.content: Thank You it's working.

entry.title: Re: Error importing Business Content: Integrated Financia...

Dear @Mr_Spock,

I'm responsible for the Integrated Financial Planning business content.
I don't know if there is any official page to review the status of this issue. I know that the root cause is already identified and my colleagues from SAC development are currently working on a fix.

As already mentioned, as soon as I get some feedback, I'll share it here.

entry.title: Re: Issue with outbound IDoc PEXR2003/PEXR2001 Missing segment and Unable to debug.

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi Olga,
Good write up!  Just connected our SAP CCM via Cloud Connector to our SAP ECC.  The connectivity setup in SAP CC was a not easy but after several tries, I was able to connect.  If we're using ECC EHP6, will ATC developer scenario work?

Thanks!
Rene

entry.title: Re: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
entry.content:
Dear Sap,

Can I use Joule for ABAP Development in ADT(Eclipse) if I follow these steps ?

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Select which Identity Provider is used for an applica...
entry.content:
Hi Rouven

I'm not sure what you want to achieve as part of this. I'd say that if you want users to login via EntraID and provide access to some PWD users the "natural" way to do it would be to configure EntraID in the app as default and set the option "allow users stored in Identity Authentication to log on". You need to provide the link to your PWD login users and make sure they have a PWD set in IAS

As an alternative, in the SAP guides you'll find complex scenarios solved via granting users to different groups in IAS. As long as your PWD users are static or you find a way to automate the group assignment in IAS (i.e via IPS) might be a valid option.

Thanks

Diego

entry.title: Re: Call an OData Web API Service externally
entry.content: how did you get your url?

entry.title: SAP CDP- Delete Activities from Customer Schema
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have a question regarding activities in CDP. Is there a way to delete activities from the Customer Schema? I am aware of an API call that allows the deletion of activities, but as stated in the documentation, it is only intended for removing activities saved to the profile. (see Screenshot)

My intention is to delete the activity itself from the CDP system. As we are currently testing the system's features, we have a few activities that need to be removed.

Thank you in advance for your help in answering this question. I appreciate your support.

Best regards,

Htet Htet

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price changing after procurement of fr...
entry.content:
Before the next fiscal/tax audit, check with the accounting and legal departments to make sure this request is legitimate. Is it a request from control, logistics or sales 😎

In this case, as suggested by @DominikTylczyn, use split valuation (Valuation Type) or even  a different plant (Valuation Area), you could (sould) even declare the new valuation record to use 'standard price' and not 'MAP'.

7m ago

entry.title: Re: ABAP custom code analysis using SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content: Very useful information Olga.  So does it mean SAP is no longer recommending the use of SYCM_DOWNLOAD_REPOSITORY_INFO for SAP S/4 HANA transformation assessments and scoping?

entry.title: Find Stock Inconsistencies Faster
entry.content:
Introducing the Display Serialized Stock Quantities app, which can help you find stock inconsistencies. Click the image below to watch the video: 

 

To learn more about how you can leverage this app, see: 

How to remove serial number inconsistencies by doing a physical inventory count.

entry.title: Re: SAP CDP- Delete Activities from Customer Schema
entry.content:
Hello @Htet,

I understand your query that you're currently testing the CDP System's features with dummy data.

  • If you want to delete activities, you can use API to delete activities associated with profiles.
  • In your case, as it's not relevant, you can create a SAP ticket to reset your Business Unit so that you can start fresh. Feel free to reply for further queries.

If it helped, mark thread as an accepted solution.
Thanks & Regards,
Kunal Bansal, SAP CX SME
https://www.linkedin.com/in/thekunalbansal

entry.title: Re: How to call a Method in a Program?
entry.content:
Hi,

an example

entry.title: Re: Issue with outbound IDoc PEXR2003/PEXR2001 Missing se...
entry.content:
Several segments are filled using user-exit functions and whether anything is executed is controlled by entries in table 'FEDICUS' (FI EDI Control for Calling Customer Functions per Segment).

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Change Currency in app 'Product profitability with Production Variance'
entry.content:
We are using the FIORI app 'Product Profitability with Production Variance,' which displays data in the controlling area currency (i.e., USD). However, we haven't found any option to change the currency in this report. We would like to view this report in the company code currency."


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Change Currency in app 'Product profitability with Pr...
entry.content:
Values will be displayed in the global currency.

Unfortunately it is not possible to change currency in this app.

Please check the SAP Help portal link for more details Product Profitability with Production Variances

entry.title: Re: Transferring Custom Pricing Field to Fiori Manage Prices (F4111)
entry.content:
Maybe try:-

Extensibility for Manage Prices - Sales - SAP Community

entry.title: Is there any standard module available to convert JSON to XML in File adapter?
entry.content:
Hi Friends, 

Good day. 

In our scenario (PIPO 7.5), it needs to pick up Json file using file sender channel and convert into XML to pass to Mapping. 

Is there any standard module available to use in channel to achieve this?

Will MessageTransformBean module support this conversion? 

Kindly clarify. 

Thank you. 

Regards, 

Jegatheeswaran P.

entry.title: Re: SAP CDP- Delete Activities from Customer Schema
entry.content: Thank you for your quick answer @KunalBansal . I understand there is an API to delete activities associated with profiles but is there still a way to delete it from the Customer Schema?(See Screenshot) If not, we will consider to reset the Business Unit. Thanks & Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Blog Series Posting Documents to SharePoint
entry.content: Can we create a Content Repository (OAC0) that points to Sharepoint site and leverage Archivelink as protocol? @TusharShinde  did you received any support to share with us? Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE error log message : Use lastsave as start poi...
entry.content: HFs provide security fixes for critical CVEs on top of the PL and should replace it. No functionality is changed - as shown by the CR list that accompanies the release. If this message has been appearing, it can probably be considered information only and benign.

entry.title: Re: Inventory Counting in SAP Business SDK
entry.content:
Hi @ANKIT_CHAUHAN,

First, thank you for this blog, a code example like this is missing from the sdk documentation.

My question is this: if I understand this correctly, this represents the Inventory Counting transaction. How can I create an Inventory Posting transaction or draw it from the Inventory Counting?

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: Re: Issue with outbound IDoc PEXR2003/PEXR2001 Missing se...
entry.content: No. No User Exits are implemented yet, atlease the exits related to this segment. So this is a complete standard idoc

entry.title: Hi, I'm not aware of a solution to your scenario other than reset of Biz Unit. Kunal
Re: FioriElements - Value-Help for SearchField - data sho...
entry.content:
check how the original annotation(regarding valuehelp) looks like

and then adapt it to your needs(disabling auto load)

entry.title: Re: Issue with outbound IDoc PEXR2003/PEXR2001 Missing se...
entry.content: @akshayrajkumar that wasn't a question, it was a statement. Several of the segments are only filled by implementing user-exits based on control entries defined in the table 'FEDICUS'.

entry.title: Re: Inventory Counting in SAP Business SDK
entry.content:
Dear Johan_Hakkesteegt,

A sample code is provided in the comments section of this blog: Inventory Posting in SAP Business One SDK 

Hope it helps!

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: How to set up Filters in AIFAEM
entry.content: @saurabhkumbhare what exactly do you mean? Changing the Language version to "ABAP for Cloud Development"? This won´t work because at the moment AIF Function Module are using TABLES-Parameters which are not allowed in this Language Model. You can define a Wrapper in Standard ABAP calling a "clean" class method though. I´ll publish a blog on this with an example very soon too.

entry.title: Re: Value help search in Smart Filterbar
entry.content: why you annotate the valuehelp's companycode? i think you have to annotate the main entity's company code.

entry.title: Re: Issue with outbound IDoc PEXR2003/PEXR2001 Missing se...
entry.content: @Ryan-Crosby Ya got it! Let me check.

entry.title: Re: Mass creation of assets
entry.content: @Joel_John, did you already try LSMW or LTMC?

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi Rene @rcsilva ,

yes, developer scenario should work. The on-premise system must have at least on SAP_BASIS 7.40. See also the blog for details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-use-abap-test-cockpit-atc-in-the-cloud-as-a-developer-in-an-on/ba-p/13574060

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to only report on the most recently mo...
entry.content: Hi Subbu, I'm looking at this but our D&I fields have no effective dated fields so this wouldn't be possible with them - especially as they both sit under global info so have the same 'edited on' date 😞

entry.title: Re: ABAP custom code analysis using SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Hi @rcsilva ,

yes, custom code analysis approach based on SYCM_DOWNLOAD_REPOSITORY_INFO is obsolete, Custom Code Migration app (based on remote ATC) should be used instead.

See also this Q&A https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/custom-code-adaptation-for-sap-s-4hana-faq/ba-p/13418880#_Toc470164251 for more details.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: In BAS, ui5-deploy.yaml files in all applications have an "Unable to load schema ..." error.
entry.content:
Out of the blue last week, in all applications that I load into BAS, the ui5-deploy.yaml is registering the following error: 

Unable to load schema from 'https://sap.github.io/ui5-tooling/schema/ui5.yaml.json': Request vscode/content failed unexpectedly without providing any details. YAML(768)

I haven't made any changes myself that would cause this for every application, but I do believe the error showed up after an SAP update to BAS. So far, research that I've done hasn't turned up any answers. Anybody have any ideas?

entry.title: Join the upcoming CEI project in 2025 for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
In this year’s Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI) project for SAP Integration Suite, we will continue collaborating with our customers and partners to gather feedback on product improvements, enhancements and new developments to validate at an early stage, and ensuring that we not only build the right features for your needs but also building it right.

This will include open discussion about our current product backlogs, roadmap items, addressing pain points and collecting feedback. Some of the core investments which we want to validate in the upcoming CEI project include Gen AI for Integration, move from SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Integration Suite, new adapters, integration in the context of Enterprise Automation, etc.

If you like to join the upcoming SAP Integration Suite influence project for providing feedback and shaping the SAP Integration Suite product, please register at the customer influence page.

Registration is open from 24th of February until 28th of March.

Once, the registration closes, we will invite you for an initial call where we go through the scope and the prerequisites of the feedback project. This will be around April so that we are able to start the project latest in May this year.

entry.title: Extract SAP Terminology (Menu, Errors, Warnings) in Multiple Languages
entry.content:
I would need to export (or extract) all terminology from SAP that appears in the menu tree, error and warning messages, etc., in German, English, and Slovenian.

Is there a way to do this, and can someone help me with it or provide the data.

entry.title: XBLNR Down payment request
entry.content: In Spain, it is necessary to report down payments with VAT. When making an Automatic Payment in SAP Public Cloud, the XBLNR field is not carried over from the down payment request. How could the field be carried out in the automatic payment execution?

entry.title: Re: Hi Team,I'm Currently working on Log On with a Custom...
entry.content:
Hi

I don't think this is possible with trial accounts. The option to establish trust was made available recently but i'm not sure if it works in trial. I've tested the scenario and I have the same issue. I've done this with a non-trial account and works as expected.

For the trial I even had to manually add the callback because it wasn't even in the list and giving an error. So as i said, it might not be possible in trial

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Create a custom MDF Object with multiple records

Hi Eugene, All
@Eugene_D_Fourie
Is there a way to allow maximum two records only in the child object? For example, currently the child accepts multiple entries but the client requires maximum two entries allowed per user in a custom mdf portlet.

entry.title: Re: Restrict only two records on Custom MDF Portlet
entry.content:
H Raviraj

Just wondering if you had a solution to this requirement? Could you please share because I'm also looking for one.

entry.title: Re: No dedicated error message found (SWF_FLEX_WEBGUI010) in SAP GUI for workflow scenario WS0200047
entry.content:
Turns out it was my primarily my own fault because I didn't catch all the exceptions after some refactoring. I suppose I should not be surprised...

But there was more to learn when I continued after catching the exception and raising one that was in the signature: Avoid raising exceptions in MMPUR_WFL_CONTEXT_ENHANCE it you can - at least when setting custom attributes or work item priority.

The journey continued. When an exception of type CX_RSM_AGT_DETN_BUSI_EXCEPTION was raised in the RSM_BADI_STATIC_RULE implementation IS_BUSINESS_EXCEPTION was set. However, it was still not perfect. The root cause was passed along via the PREVIOUS exception, but in ME53N the message displayed was now "An exception was raised". Passing along the text ID of the previous exception was not a good solution either, because only the message definition text was displayed - the parameter values were lost.

I have debugged for several hours, into the following classes/methods and probably some more, and unfortunately my conclusion is that it isn't possible to pass on a message with parameters. In one of these methods (can't recall exactly where it was now) the "business exception" is copied from the exception, but it is only the text ID that is copied - I could not find any attempt to retrieve parameter values.

CL_RSM_QUERY_ENGINE > IF_RSM_QUERY_ENGINE~EXECUTE_RULE_IMPLEMENTATION
CL_RSM_QUERY_ENGINE > IF_RSM_QUERY_ENGINE~INVOKE_QUERY_ENGINE
CL_RSM_DETERMINE_RESPONSIBLE > IF_RSM_DETERMINE_RESPONSIBLE~DETERMINE_RESPONSIBLE
CL_SWF_FLEX_UTL_RESPY_RULE > IF_SWF_FLEX_UTL_RESPY_RULE~GET_USERS_FOR_RESPY_RULE
CL_SWF_FLEX_RUN_RESPY_RULE > IF_SWF_FLEX_RUN_AGENT~EVALUATE
CL_SWF_FLEX_RUN_AGENT > IF_SWF_FLEX_RUN_AGENT~EVALUATE
CL_SWF_FLEX_RUN_AGENT > RAISE_NO_AGENT_EXCEPTION
CL_SWF_FLEX_RUN_WORKFLOW > IF_SWF_FLEX_ACTIVITY~RESOLVE_AGENTS
CL_SWF_FLEX_ENGINE_ACTIVITY > EXECUTE (LCL_ON_NO_AGENT_FOUND_CALLER)
CL_MM_PUR_PR_FLEX_WFL > IF_SWF_FLEX_IFS_RUN_APPL~ON_NO_AGENT_FOUND

In the end I created a new message stating simply that an approver can't be found, with a long text telling the administrator that there should be additional information in the workflow log to tell you the root cause. I can't say I feel satisfied, but sometimes defeat has to be accepted.

From an end user perspective this new message - compared to "An exception was raised" - is at least saying something about what the problem is and how to proceed.

entry.title: How to change target mapping parameters for a fiori app?
entry.content:
Hello,

We want to use the F1511A - Create Maintenance Request app for creating notification type M1 - Maintenance Request. But as mentioned in the target mapping of the app - Notification type = Y1 desn't allow to create such notification.


If we try to modify the target mapping parameter values using fiori launchpad designer - the system shows the following warning. And it will break the reference with the tile and in the technical catalog everything gets deleted.


Does anyone have an idea, how to proceed with the such customization?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price changing after procurement of fr...
entry.content: A separate valuation area (plant) for free goods might be an option, however side effects have to be considered, e.g. increased master data volume, MRP planning plans on plant (MRP area) level so it won't consolidate regular stock and free goods.

entry.title: Re: Blog Series Posting Documents to SharePoint
entry.content:
Hi Luiz - Sorry I haven't received any support on this topic but then we had eventually built a product that works seamlessly with SAP and Microsoft Sharepoint. If you are interested in knowing more please feel free to reach out to us using the contact form (you can quote this link or my name while reaching out)  - https://versafile.com/docuflow/

Regards

Tushar

entry.title: Re: SOA-Management - MDG - Data Replication - SRT_CORE122 - Authorization missing
entry.content: Hallo Patrick, do you have a solution here? Thank you very much Anika

entry.title: Re: Screen Personas Slipstream engine – Footer Buttons no...
entry.content:
Webgui and Slipstream menu and toolbars structure can be different. If you will prepare Flavor in Webgui and consume in SE there could be some inconsistency also.

In case of Webgui [Save, Cancel, Put in Process, Postpone, Complete] are displayed in Status Bar.
In case of SE [Save, Cancel] are in Status Bar and [Put in Process, Postpone, Complete] in Toolbar.

Anyway, if Desktop mode of SE has [Save, Cancel] buttons in Status Bar, then these buttons must appear in expanded mode of Mobile Menu Button "...". Compare Original Screen for that case. 



If some button is needed from Toolbar, you can drag it into the Status Bar and it will appear in expanded mode of Mobile Menu Button also.



Further inconsistency of Flavor prepared in Webgui and consumed in SE must be examined and corrected manually.

Best Regards!

entry.title: Re: Error: Fiori tool guided development with OData service v2
entry.content: Hi, could you provide more details on the OData v2 service you were using... Also what is the error details in the Application Modeler Output channel?

I am trying to understand what you are describing. Is there a KBA tied to this?

entry.title: Unable pass ODN number in reference field in accounting document.
entry.content:
Dear Member ,

we have created condition contract , we have generated settlement document after executing T- code WB2R_SC , credit memo is generated , Now settlement document number is passing as reference number in accounting .

we have to pass OND number in reference number in  accounting document .  

what configuration need to pass ODN number after settlement of condition contract .

Kindly suggest .

Thanks & Regards ,

Shital

entry.title: Re: DTW- Outgoing Payment Upload - Journal Entry
entry.content:
Hi @Thaonguyenphuong ,

Try adding the field InvType to the VPM2 template and use the Journal Entry object in there (30). I think SAP displays this message because it simply doesn't know which object you are referring to.

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Joule Custom Term Recognition
entry.content: With SF Joule I saw previously a roadmap item for 'Custom Term Recognition' for H2 2024. I did not see this delivered in H2 2024 is this still coming and when?

entry.title: Re: Onboarding Dashboard (New) in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding: H2 2020
entry.content:
Hi there,

Does anyone know if it's possible for documents uploaded during the Review New Hire Data step (hiring manager) via a Custom MDF portlet which is added to the Hiring Manager Template - whether these documents can be configured to upload and store in the Onboarding Documents on the Dashboard? Or is that storage location only for documents uploaded during the Additional Data Collection?

Many thanks,
